steps.title: Spot the myth-busting structure
steps.content: The passage opens by stating that animals use tools, then explicitly challenges the old belief that tool use was unique to primates, claiming this myth has been “busted” by evidence of diverse animals using tools (elephants, woodpecker finches).

steps.title: Match the structure to the main purpose
steps.content: Choice D directly captures the passage's structure: arguing that a variety of animals use tools. The other choices either invent claims absent from the passage (like intelligence) or misread the scope—focusing on narrow comparisons instead of the broader argument about multiple animal types using tools. That matches choice D — D is correct.
